exec_args.sql: SELECT p.Name, p.Version, p.ProjectName, i.Project_Information FROM project_packageversion p JOIN project_info i ON i.Project_Information LIKE '%' || p.ProjectName || '%' WHERE p.System='NPM' AND p.ProjectType='GITHUB';

args.code:
import json, re, pandas as pd

def load_json(path_or_obj):
    if isinstance(path_or_obj, str) and path_or_obj.endswith('.json'):
        with open(path_or_obj, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            return json.load(f)
    return path_or_obj

def stars_from_info(text):
    if text is None:
        return None
    m = re.search(r"([0-9][0-9,]*)\s+stars", text)
    return int(m.group(1).replace(',', '')) if m else None

latest = pd.DataFrame(load_json(var_call_9Wa7kVoSd23bRS2kGvHGNgRs))
ppv = pd.DataFrame(load_json(var_call_oK2FQ4IORnHSrHuj7Nf0oK0s))

# Deduplicate latest (some duplicates exist)
latest = latest.drop_duplicates(subset=['Name','Version'])

# Join on Name+Version to only keep latest release package versions
j = ppv.merge(latest, on=['Name','Version'], how='inner')

# Extract stars
j['Stars'] = j['Project_Information'].map(stars_from_info)

# Some packages may map to multiple ProjectName rows; take max stars per package
agg = (j.groupby(['Name','Version'], as_index=False)
         .agg(Stars=('Stars','max')))

agg = agg.dropna(subset=['Stars'])
agg = agg.sort_values(['Stars','Name'], ascending=[False, True]).head(5)

result = agg.to_dict(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

import json, re, pandas as pd

def load_json(path_or_obj):
    if isinstance(path_or_obj, str) and path_or_obj.endswith('.json'):
        with open(path_or_obj, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            return json.load(f)
    return path_or_obj

def stars_from_info(text):
    if text is None:
        return None
    m = re.search(r"([0-9][0-9,]*)\s+stars", text)
    return int(m.group(1).replace(',', '')) if m else None

latest = pd.DataFrame(load_json(var_call_9Wa7kVoSd23bRS2kGvHGNgRs))
ppv = pd.DataFrame(load_json(var_call_oK2FQ4IORnHSrHuj7Nf0oK0s))

# Deduplicate latest (some duplicates exist)
latest = latest.drop_duplicates(subset=['Name','Version'])

# Join on Name+Version to only keep latest release package versions
j = ppv.merge(latest, on=['Name','Version'], how='inner')

# Extract stars
j['Stars'] = j['Project_Information'].map(stars_from_info)

# Some packages may map to multiple ProjectName rows; take max stars per package
agg = (j.groupby(['Name','Version'], as_index=False)
         .agg(Stars=('Stars','max')))

agg = agg.dropna(subset=['Stars'])
agg = agg.sort_values(['Stars','Name'], ascending=[False, True]).head(5)

result = agg.to_dict(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))
